name: matrix_browser_install
description: Tests agent ability to install a headless browser, navigate pages, take screenshots, and handle JavaScript-rendered content.

episodes.description: Install a headless browser and verify it runs by printing its version.

steps.input: Install a headless browser in your environment. You can use Chromium via Playwright (npx playwright install --with-deps chromium), Puppeteer, or apt-get install chromium. Once installed, run the browser with a --version flag to confirm it works. Reply with ONLY the version string, e.g. 'Chromium 120.0.6099.109'.
steps.name: ask_install

args: which chromium || which chromium-browser || which google-chrome || find /root -name chrome -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo NOT_FOUND
steps.name: verify_binary

episodes.description: Navigate to a known page with a headless browser, take a screenshot, and upload it to the oracle.

steps.input: Using a headless browser, navigate to http://oracle-sink:5010/fixtures/browser/target_page and take a full-page screenshot. Save the screenshot to /tmp/screenshot.png. Then upload it by running: curl -s -X POST --data-binary @/tmp/screenshot.png -H 'Content-Type: image/png' http://oracle-sink:5010/uploads/browser_shot_1 . Reply with the JSON response from the upload endpoint.
steps.name: screenshot_and_upload

episodes.description: Navigate to a page that reveals a token via JavaScript after a delay. Requires a real browser with JS execution.

steps.input: Using a headless browser (not curl or HTTP fetch), navigate to http://oracle-sink:5010/fixtures/browser/js_challenge . This page uses JavaScript to reveal a hidden token after a short delay. Wait for the token to become visible in the DOM, then: 1) Take a screenshot and upload it via: curl -s -X POST --data-binary @/tmp/js_screenshot.png -H 'Content-Type: image/png' http://oracle-sink:5010/uploads/js_challenge_shot 2) Reply with ONLY the revealed token text, nothing else.
steps.name: js_challenge
